name: Linguine with Greens, Potato, Garlic, Hot Pepper & Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Juice from the tomatoes or Tomato juice
4 cups Assorted greens (escarole, Arugula, dandelion, chard)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bake the potatoes in a 350°F oven for 35-40 minutes, until tender but firm. Or steam them in water or vegetable stock to cover. If steaming in vegetable stock, reserve it for the pasta sauce.
2. When cool enough to handle, cut the potatoes into 1-inch chunks.
3. If including green beans, cook them in vegetable stock and immediately refresh in cold water.
4. Drop the pasta into a large kettle of boiling salted water. Stir.
5. Heat the 1/2 cup stock in a saute pan over moderate heat. Add the onions and cook, covered, until tender, about 10 minutes.
6. Add the garlic and hot red pepper flakes and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
7. Add the tomatoes, tomato liquid, potatoes, and greens (and beans, if using) to the sauce. Cook until the greens are wilted and the potatoes are heated through.
8. Season with salt and pepper.
9. When the pasta is al dente, drain it and toss with the sauce in a warmed bowl.
Note: Canned tomatoes can be substituted for fresh.
